Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE 1: Search Slack messages with a query related to "6.25" or "conventional 30yr" or "rate drop" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on #rate-watch (channel ID: C005) to find Camille Foster's post (user_id: keystone_081307e9502b) announcing that conventional 30yr rates came down to 6.25%, mentioning that recent closings above 7% should be contacted, and tagging Tomas.
OE 2: Search emails with a query related to "refi" or "rate drop" or "outreach" (or similar) using search_emails to find Grace Yamamoto's email (email_inject_refi_003) from grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com to Tomas instructing him to build a targeted refi outreach list for borrowers who closed in the last 12 months at 7% or above, check post-close history before outreach, and pay extra attention to Marcus Webb's former clients.
OE 3: Search emails with a query related to "escrow" or "Brenda Nelson" (or similar) using search_emails to find Jasmine Brooks's forwarded email (email_inject_refi_002) from jasmine.brooks@keystonemortgage.com to Tomas about Brenda Nelson, who closed LN-2025-00446 at 7.375% and is upset about escrow increasing from ~$2,450 to $2,847/mo, noting this could be a refi opportunity given rates at 6.25%.
OE 4: Search Slack messages with a query related to "servicer" or "delinquent" or "payment applied" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on #loan-processing (channel ID: C002) to find Keith Langford's post (user_id: keystone_e71e4f1f2242) about Rachel Chen on LN-2025-00519 whose April payment was applied to principal only, servicer showing her as delinquent on interest portion.
OE 5: Search emails with a query related to "Rachel Chen" or "payment problem" (or similar) using search_emails to find Rachel Chen's email (email_inject_refi_004) from rachel.chen@yahoo.com to Natasha Okafor describing the servicer payment misapplication, late fee issue, and asking about refinancing since her rate is 7.625% vs 6.25% available.
OE 6: Search emails with a query related to "Rachel Chen" (or similar) using search_emails to find Natasha Okafor's internal email (email_inject_refi_005) from natasha.okafor@keystonemortgage.com to Keith Langford confirming the payment issue is a servicer error and recommending Tomas be looped in for refi since Rachel's rate is 7.625%.
OE 7: Search the LOS for closed loans with high rates using mortgage_los_search_loans (multiple searches with pagination may be needed) to identify borrowers who closed within roughly the last 12 months at rates of 7% or higher. Qualifying borrowers include: Brenda Nelson (LN-2025-00446, rate 7.375%, closed 2025-11-04, loan amount $352,800), Rachel Chen (LN-2025-00519, rate 7.625%, closed 2026-02-05, loan amount $238,500), Victor Taylor (LN-2025-00333, rate 7.375%, closed 2025-06-24, loan amount $345,800, Marcus Webb's client), and Charles Martin (LN-2025-00332, rate 7.25%, closed 2025-06-26, loan amount $348,500).
OE 8: Look up individual loan details for each qualifying borrower using mortgage_los_get_loan or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id to retrieve full loan details including rate, loan amount, property address, and closing date needed to calculate current monthly payment vs refi payment at 6.25%.
OE 9: Look up borrower contact information for each qualifying borrower using mortgage_los_search_borrowers or mortgage_los_get_borrower_by_id to retrieve email addresses and contact info needed for personalized outreach emails: brenda.nelson@hotmail.com, rachel.chen@yahoo.com, victor.taylor@gmail.com, charles.martin@gmail.com.
OE 10: Search the CRM with a query related to "Brenda Nelson" (or similar) using crm_search_contacts then crm_list_engagements (contact_ids including crm_crmcontact_inject_bnelson) to find CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_005 documenting her escrow increase complaint where her payment went from ~$2,450 to $2,847 due to escrow adjustment, asking about refi given rate of 7.375% vs 6.25%, forwarded by Jasmine to Tomas. Brenda has an open issue that must be acknowledged in outreach.
OE 11: Search the CRM with a query related to "Rachel Chen" (or similar) using crm_search_contacts then crm_list_engagements (contact_ids including crm_crmcontact_inject_rchen) to find CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_004 documenting the servicer payment misapplication where the April payment was applied to principal only, interest showing delinquent, borrower called twice with no resolution, rate 7.625%, asking about refinancing. High retention risk requiring the servicer issue to be addressed before or alongside refi outreach.
OE 12: Search the CRM with a query related to "Victor Taylor" (or similar) using crm_search_contacts then crm_list_engagements (contact_ids including crm_crmcontact_inject_taylor) to find two critical engagements: (1) crm_engagement_inject_refi_001 (NOTE, dated 2025-06-24) where Marcus told Victor at closing that if rates dropped below 6.75% in 6-8 months Keystone would proactively reach out about a no-cost refinance, and (2) crm_engagement_inject_refi_002 (CALL, dated 2026-04-24) where Victor called asking about the rate review Marcus promised, sees 6.25% advertised, nobody contacted him, frustrated and getting competing mailers. This is a Marcus-era unfulfilled commitment that must be honored.
OE 13: Search the CRM with a query related to "Charles Martin" (or similar) using crm_search_contacts then crm_list_engagements (contact_ids including crm_crmcontact_inject_martin) to find CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_003 (NOTE, dated 2025-08-15) documenting that Charles Martin on LN-2025-00332 was charged $525 for appraisal fee via Stripe but the actual cost from Carolina Appraisal Group was only $475, a $50 difference that was supposed to be refunded per Keisha but never happened. Charles's loan was assigned to Keisha Williams (los_staff_9f0f19845970), not Marcus, but the fee discrepancy is an unresolved issue.
OE 14: Search Stripe for Charles Martin's appraisal charge using stripe_search_customers (query: "Charles Martin" or similar) or stripe_list_charges (customer_id: "cus_0oFzHw8JRhDQiT88") to find charge ch_BtpBjpmS1zkc3Gji: "Appraisal fee -- Martin (LN-2025-00332)" for $525.00 (52500 cents), confirming the $50 overcharge vs the $475 actual appraisal cost documented in the CRM note.
OE 15: Search QuickBooks for the appraisal bill related to Charles Martin / LN-2025-00332 using quickbooks_search_bills or quickbooks_search_vendors (query: "Carolina Valuation Services" or similar, vendor V-CAROLINA-002). The agent may not find a specific bill for LN-2025-00332; the $475 actual appraisal cost is documented in CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_003, which the agent should compare against the Stripe charge of $525 to confirm the $50 overcharge.
OE 16: Check LOS staff records using mortgage_los_list_staff to identify Marcus Webb (los_staff_a583f044387a), role: loan_officer, email: marcus.webb@keystonemortgage.com. His is_active flag still shows true in the LOS, but the prompt indicates he "left a few weeks ago." The agent should note Marcus's staff ID to cross-reference which qualifying borrowers were his clients.
OE 17: Cross-reference which refi-eligible borrowers were assigned to Marcus Webb using mortgage_los_search_loans or mortgage_los_get_loan_by_id, checking the assigned_lo field against Marcus Webb's staff ID (los_staff_a583f044387a). Victor Taylor's loan LN-2025-00333 was assigned to Marcus Webb, and the CRM engagements confirm Marcus made rate review commitments that were never followed up on after his departure.
OE 18: Search emails and Slack for additional borrower issues on qualifying refi candidates using search_emails and conversations_search_messages (query: borrower names or loan numbers) to find any mentions, complaints, or unresolved issues beyond the Brenda Nelson, Rachel Chen, and Victor Taylor issues already found. Any newly discovered issues should inform whether the borrower gets a standard refi pitch or an issue-acknowledging outreach.
OE 19: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient brenda.nelson@hotmail.com using send_email, acknowledging her escrow increase frustration (payment going from ~$2,450 to $2,847), explaining how refinancing at 6.25% could lower her rate from 7.375%, and offering to help with the refi process.
OE 20: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient rachel.chen@yahoo.com using send_email, first acknowledging the servicer payment misapplication issue and offering help resolving it, then discussing the refi opportunity at 6.25% vs her current 7.625%.
OE 21: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient victor.taylor@gmail.com using send_email, acknowledging Marcus's departure and the unfulfilled rate review commitment (Marcus promised proactive outreach if rates dropped below 6.75%), that Keystone failed to follow through, and that rates are now at 6.25% making a refi worthwhile.
OE 22: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient charles.martin@gmail.com using send_email, addressing the unresolved appraisal fee overcharge ($50 difference) and presenting the refi opportunity at 6.25% vs his current 7.25%.
OE 23: Send standard refi outreach emails to any additional qualifying borrowers (closed within ~12 months at 7%+ rate) who do NOT have open complaints or issues using send_email (sender: tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com) with a standard refi pitch presenting estimated savings from their current rate to 6.25%.
OE 24: Log a CRM engagement for each borrower outreach using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "EMAIL" or "NOTE", body describing the refi outreach email sent and any issue acknowledgment, contact_ids linked to the borrower's CRM contact ID) for Brenda Nelson, Rachel Chen, Victor Taylor, Charles Martin, and any additional qualifying borrowers.
OE 25: Add a loan activity note for Brenda Nelson's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3d3656668645" for LN-2025-00446, action: "note") documenting the escrow increase from ~$2,450 to $2,847, borrower frustration, and refi email sent.
OE 26: Add a loan activity note for Rachel Chen's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_12422f01fffc" for LN-2025-00519, action: "note") documenting the servicer error with April payment applied to principal only showing delinquent, and refi email sent.
OE 27: Add a loan activity note for Victor Taylor's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_4ddc6f289059" for LN-2025-00333, action: "note") documenting Marcus Webb's unfulfilled promise of proactive outreach if rates dropped below 6.75%, Keystone's failure to follow up, and refi email sent.
OE 28: Add a loan activity note for Charles Martin's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_34763a865f4b" for LN-2025-00332, action: "note") documenting the appraisal fee overcharge of $50 (Stripe charge of $525 vs $475 actual cost), pending refund, and refi email sent.
OE 29: Issue a Stripe refund for Charles Martin's appraisal fee overcharge using stripe_create_refund (charge_id: "ch_BtpBjpmS1zkc3Gji", amount: 5000, reason: "requested_by_customer" or similar) for the $50 difference between the $525 Stripe charge and the $475 actual appraisal cost stated in CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_003. This is a proactive action the agent may take based on the prompt's instruction to address fee discrepancies that should have been handled.
OE 30: Send an email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com using send_email to deliver the complete refi campaign breakdown including the full list of qualifying borrowers, their current rates vs 6.25% refi rate, estimated monthly savings for each, which borrowers had open issues (Brenda Nelson - escrow, Rachel Chen - servicer error, Victor Taylor - unfulfilled Marcus commitment, Charles Martin - appraisal overcharge), what was done to address each, and confirmation that personalized emails were sent.
OE 31: Post a summary in Slack #sales-pipeline using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") with a payload summarizing how many borrowers were contacted, the rate opportunity (current rates at 7%+ vs 6.25% refi), which borrowers have open issues that loan officers should be aware of, and a note that personalized emails have been sent so loan officers know what went out and who might be calling in.

sandbox_idvm-a501fdbd6f9f4e1f
universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 28, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmortgage-broker-01-keystone

About Keystone Mortgage Partners: Keystone is a residential mortgage brokerage in Charlotte, NC. The company brokers loans through wholesale lenders. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Mortgage LOS: Loan origination system — loans, borrowers, conditions, document checklists, milestones, activity logs, staff, lenders, vendors - Stripe: Payment processing — borrower deposits and closing costs, appraisal fee charges, vendor transfers via connected accounts, bank account verification via Financial Connections - CRM: Sales pipeline — leads, prospects, engagement notes, contact management - QuickBooks: Accounting — vendor bills (appraisers, rate lock fees), invoices, customer records - Filesystem: Loan document folders — PDFs of W-2s, tax returns, appraisals, loan estimates, closing disclosures - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.

OE 1: Search Slack messages with a query related to "6.25" or "conventional 30yr" or "rate drop" (or similar) using conversations_search_messages or conversations_history on #rate-watch (channel ID: C005) to find Camille Foster's post (user_id: keystone_081307e9502b) announcing that conventional 30yr rates came down to 6.25%, mentioning that recent closings above 7% should be contacted, and tagging Tomas.

OE 18: Search emails and Slack for additional borrower issues on qualifying refi candidates using search_emails and conversations_search_messages (query: borrower names or loan numbers) to find any mentions, complaints, or unresolved issues beyond the Brenda Nelson, Rachel Chen, and Victor Taylor issues already found. Any newly discovered issues should inform whether the borrower gets a standard refi pitch or an issue-acknowledging outreach.
OE 19: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient brenda.nelson@hotmail.com using send_email, acknowledging her escrow increase frustration (payment going from ~$2,450 to $2,847), explaining how refinancing at 6.25% could lower her rate from 7.375%, and offering to help with the refi process.
OE 20: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient rachel.chen@yahoo.com using send_email, first acknowledging the servicer payment misapplication issue and offering help resolving it, then discussing the refi opportunity at 6.25% vs her current 7.625%.
OE 21: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient victor.taylor@gmail.com using send_email, acknowledging Marcus's departure and the unfulfilled rate review commitment (Marcus promised proactive outreach if rates dropped below 6.75%), that Keystone failed to follow through, and that rates are now at 6.25% making a refi worthwhile.
OE 22: Send a personalized refi email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient charles.martin@gmail.com using send_email, addressing the unresolved appraisal fee overcharge ($50 difference) and presenting the refi opportunity at 6.25% vs his current 7.25%.
OE 23: Send standard refi outreach emails to any additional qualifying borrowers (closed within ~12 months at 7%+ rate) who do NOT have open complaints or issues using send_email (sender: tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com) with a standard refi pitch presenting estimated savings from their current rate to 6.25%.
OE 24: Log a CRM engagement for each borrower outreach using crm_create_engagement (engagement_type: "EMAIL" or "NOTE", body describing the refi outreach email sent and any issue acknowledgment, contact_ids linked to the borrower's CRM contact ID) for Brenda Nelson, Rachel Chen, Victor Taylor, Charles Martin, and any additional qualifying borrowers.
OE 25: Add a loan activity note for Brenda Nelson's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_3d3656668645" for LN-2025-00446, action: "note") documenting the escrow increase from ~$2,450 to $2,847, borrower frustration, and refi email sent.
OE 26: Add a loan activity note for Rachel Chen's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_12422f01fffc" for LN-2025-00519, action: "note") documenting the servicer error with April payment applied to principal only showing delinquent, and refi email sent.
OE 27: Add a loan activity note for Victor Taylor's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_4ddc6f289059" for LN-2025-00333, action: "note") documenting Marcus Webb's unfulfilled promise of proactive outreach if rates dropped below 6.75%, Keystone's failure to follow up, and refi email sent.
OE 28: Add a loan activity note for Charles Martin's loan using mortgage_los_add_activity (loan_id: "los_loan_34763a865f4b" for LN-2025-00332, action: "note") documenting the appraisal fee overcharge of $50 (Stripe charge of $525 vs $475 actual cost), pending refund, and refi email sent.
OE 29: Issue a Stripe refund for Charles Martin's appraisal fee overcharge using stripe_create_refund (charge_id: "ch_BtpBjpmS1zkc3Gji", amount: 5000, reason: "requested_by_customer" or similar) for the $50 difference between the $525 Stripe charge and the $475 actual appraisal cost stated in CRM engagement crm_engagement_inject_refi_003. This is a proactive action the agent may take based on the prompt's instruction to address fee discrepancies that should have been handled.
OE 30: Send an email with sender tomas.herrera@keystonemortgage.com and recipient grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com using send_email to deliver the complete refi campaign breakdown including the full list of qualifying borrowers, their current rates vs 6.25% refi rate, estimated monthly savings for each, which borrowers had open issues (Brenda Nelson - escrow, Rachel Chen - servicer error, Victor Taylor - unfulfilled Marcus commitment, Charles Martin - appraisal overcharge), what was done to address each, and confirmation that personalized emails were sent.
OE 31: Post a summary in Slack #sales-pipeline using conversations_add_message (channel_id: "C006") with a payload summarizing how many borrowers were contacted, the rate opportunity (current rates at 7%+ vs 6.25% refi), which borrowers have open issues that loan officers should be aware of, and a note that personalized emails have been sent so loan officers know what went out and who might be calling in.

✓Run #4The email to Grace contains the full campaign breakdown including per-borrower savings figures (e.g., ~$264/mo, ~$451/mo, etc.) and current rate vs 6.25% refi comparisons for all 30 qualifying borrowers.

✓Run #6The email to Grace was described as including 'all 30 borrowers with rates, savings calculations' and individual emails contained rate comparisons (e.g., 7.625% → 6.25%, ~$220/mo savings for Rachel Chen; 7.375% → 6.25%, ~$265/mo for Brenda Nelson, etc.), which were included in the breakdown.
20 The email content (visible in send_email or reply_to_email parameters) sent to [grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com](mailto:grace.yamamoto@keystonemortgage.com) identifies which borrowers had open issues, including Brenda Nelson (escrow), Rachel Chen (servicer error), and Victor Taylor (Marcus's unfulfilled commitment).
outcome
 ✓✗✗✓✗✓ 50%

✓Run #4The email to Grace explicitly identifies issue borrowers including Brenda Nelson (escrow complaint), Rachel Chen (servicer payment misapplication), and Victor Taylor (Marcus Webb's unfulfilled rate review commitment), separating them from clean borrowers.
✗Run #5The email to Grace identifies Brenda Nelson (escrow) and Rachel Chen (servicer error) as issue borrowers, but does NOT include Victor Taylor (LN-2025-00333) with Marcus's unfulfilled commitment. Instead, James Walker was substituted as the Marcus Webb client. Victor Taylor was never found or mentioned.
✓Run #6The Grace email breakdown included issue flags, and the CRM engagements and LOS notes all documented Brenda Nelson (escrow), Rachel Chen (servicer error), and Victor Taylor (Marcus Webb commitment) as issue borrowers — these would be reflected in the Grace breakdown email.

✗Run #6Charles Martin was never identified as having an issue. The agent sent him a standard refi pitch with no mention of the appraisal overcharge, created no CRM engagement for him, added no LOS note, and did not flag him in the Grace email. He would appear only as a clean outreach borrower.

outcome

✗Run #2The final response identifies James Walker as Marcus Webb's client, not Victor Taylor. Victor Taylor (LN-2025-00333, 7.375%) with the unfulfilled rate-review promise (rates below 6.75%) is absent from the entire trajectory and final response.
✗Run #3The final response explicitly states 'None of the 10 qualifying borrowers were assigned to Marcus as LO' and does not identify Victor Taylor at all. Victor Taylor (LN-2025-00333) was missed entirely by the agent.
✓Run #4The final response explicitly states: 'Victor Taylor (7.375%) — Marcus Webb promised him a proactive rate-review refi if rates dropped below 6.75%. Nobody ever called.' This directly identifies Victor as a former Marcus Webb client with the unfulfilled commitment.
✗Run #5Victor Taylor (LN-2025-00333, 7.375%) was never identified in the trajectory. The agent found James Walker (LN-2026-00596, 7.053%) as Marcus Webb's former client instead. There is no mention of Victor Taylor's unfulfilled rate review promise (rates below 6.75%) anywhere.
✓Run #6The final response explicitly identifies 'Victor Taylor — Marcus Webb's unfulfilled rate review promise apologized for (high-priority retention)'. The CRM engagement (item 250) and LOS note (item 280) confirm the agent found and acted on the specific rate review commitment.

query construction

✓Run #3Tool Call 8 uses query 'closed', Tool Call 13 uses 'LN-2025', Tool Calls 20-21 use '7.375' and '7.25' — collectively these queries target closed loans with high rates to find qualifying refi candidates.
✓Run #4The first mortgage_los_search_loans call (index [18]) used query 'closed' to find closed loans, then subsequent calls paginated through all loans using 'LN-2025' queries. The queries are designed to return loans from the current period.
✓Run #5The agent called mortgage_los_search_loans with queries '2025' (for loans from that year) and 'closed' (for closed loan status), which are terms that would return closed loans with relevant rate information for identifying refi candidates.
✓Run #6mortgage_los_search_loans was called with query='closed' (limit=50) and 'LN-2025' (with multiple offsets) — terms that would return closed loans with recent loan numbers to identify qualifying refi candidates.

query construction
 ✓✓✗✓✓✗ 67%

The criterion requires a specific search query matching those terms; the agent only fetched general channel history.
✓Run #4The agent made conversations_search_messages calls with queries including 'servicer payment delinquent', 'botched payment' targeting #loan-processing (C002), and 'Rachel Chen' — all terms that would match Keith Langford's post about the servicer payment misapplication.
✓Run #5The agent used conversations_history on the #loan-processing channel (which would capture Keith Langford's post about Rachel Chen's servicer payment misapplication). While conversations_search_messages wasn't used specifically for this, reading the full channel history achieves equivalent coverage for finding the servicer discussion.
